players.name: Yassine Bounou
players.bio: Born in Montreal, 'Bono' returns to his birth country for the World Cup — a full-circle moment. The Al-Hilal stopper was the hero of Qatar 2022, saving two penalties vs. Spain. A colossus between the sticks with 90 caps.
players.facts: Born in Canada, playing against Canada is theoretically possible. A walking conflict of interest.

players.name: Achraf Hakimi
players.full_name: Achraf Hakimi Mouh; أشرف حكيمي موح
players.bio: One of the world's elite full-backs, Hakimi was born in Madrid yet terrorises it for fun. A jet-heeled overlapper with 11 international goals, he was the heartbeat of Morocco's historic Qatar 2022 semi-final run.
players.facts: His 'Hakimi' nutmeg celebration after scoring vs. Spain in 2022 broke Moroccan social media entirely.

players.name: Noussair Mazraoui
players.bio: A Dutch-born Moroccan who made Man United his home, Mazraoui brings composure and versatility to the backline. Capable at right or left back, he adds European pedigree with 45 caps for the Atlas Lions.
players.facts: He chose Morocco over the Netherlands — a decision Dutch fans still haven't quite forgiven.

players.name: Sofyan Amrabat
players.bio: The engine room of Morocco's midfield, Amrabat became a global sensation at Qatar 2022 with his relentless pressing and composure under pressure. Now at Real Betis, the Huizen-born destroyer has 75 caps and zero goals — he's not paid to score.
players.facts: His bandana became an iconic fashion statement at Qatar 2022. Searches for 'Amrabat bandana' spiked 4,000%.

players.name: Marwane Saâdane
players.bio: A seasoned centre-back with Moroccan roots, Saâdane brings veteran experience from his time in Saudi Arabia. With only 8 caps, the World Cup represents a huge stage for the 34-year-old Mohammedia native to shine.
players.facts: At 34, he's proof that it's never too late to get your World Cup shot. Better late than never, Marwane.

players.name: Ayyoub Bouaddi
players.bio: Born in 2007 — the year the iPhone launched — Bouaddi is frighteningly young. The Lille midfielder earned his first caps as a teenager and arrives at this World Cup as one of the tournament's most exciting teen prospects.
players.facts: Bouaddi was born the same year Lionel Messi won his first Ballon d'Or. He was 18 when named in this squad.

players.name: Chemsdine Talbi
players.bio: The Belgian-born winger swapped Sunderland's Black Cat badge for the Atlas Lion at international level. Still only 21, Talbi is raw but exciting, bringing pace and directness to Morocco's right flank.
players.facts: Plays in the English Championship for Sunderland — making him one of very few second-tier players at WC 2026.

players.name: Azzedine Ounahi
players.bio: A dynamic box-to-box midfielder with an eye for goal, Ounahi caught the world's attention at Qatar 2022 from relative obscurity. Now at Girona, he's added consistency to his undeniable flair — 9 goals in 49 caps backs it up.
players.facts: Was virtually unknown before Qatar 2022. He went from Angers bench-warmer to global stardom in two weeks.

players.name: Soufiane Rahimi
players.bio: A prolific striker who has flourished in the UAE with Al Ain, Rahimi brings sharp movement and a clinical finish. With 12 goals in 37 caps, the 30-year-old knows where the net is — and he's not shy about using it.
players.facts: Rahimi won the AFC Champions League with Al Ain in 2024, netting key goals throughout the campaign.

players.name: Brahim Díaz
players.bio: Born in Málaga, raised at Manchester City, and sharpened at Real Madrid — Brahim Díaz's career reads like a roadmap of European football. Chose Morocco over Spain and has rewarded them handsomely: 14 goals in just 26 caps.
players.facts: Scored on his Morocco debut and never looked back. Spain's loss was very much Morocco's gain.

players.name: Ismael Saibari
players.bio: A creative and energetic midfielder who has been central to PSV's success in the Eredivisie. Saibari brings clever movement and goals from midfield, with an impressive 9 strikes in 31 international appearances.
players.facts: Saibari helped PSV win the Dutch Eredivisie title and has been one of the league's standout midfielders.

players.name: Munir Mohamedi
players.bio: The veteran backup keeper with 53 caps, Mohamedi has served Morocco faithfully for over a decade. At 37, he is the squad's elder statesman between the sticks — experienced, reliable, and probably very good at crosswords.
players.facts: At 37, he's the oldest player in Morocco's squad and has been keeping goal for his country since 2012.

players.name: Zakaria El Ouahdi
players.bio: A New Year's Eve baby born in Antwerp, El Ouahdi is a young right-back developing nicely at Genk. With just 3 caps to his name, this World Cup is a giant leap forward for the 24-year-old Belgian-born defender.
players.facts: Born on 31 December — meaning he's always the last to celebrate his birthday. Timing, as ever, is everything.

players.name: Issa Diop
players.full_name: Issa Laye Lucas Jean Diop; عيسى ديوب
players.bio: Born in Toulouse, Issa Diop is a towering centre-back who has carved out a solid Premier League career at Fulham. The French-born defender chose Morocco for international duty — and brings imposing physicality to the backline.
players.facts: Diop is 6ft 4in of pure defensive muscle. Strikers have reportedly filed formal complaints.

players.name: Samir El Mourabet
players.bio: A homegrown Strasbourg product born in the city he represents professionally, El Mourabet is one of Morocco's most exciting young midfielders. Still only 20, his 4 caps hint at a long international career ahead.
players.facts: Born and raised in Strasbourg, he plays for Strasbourg. A man of simple, local loyalties — except he plays for Morocco.

players.name: Gessime Yassine
players.bio: Another Strasbourg lad making the most of his chance, Gessime Yassine is a pacy right winger who brings energy off the bench. Born in Salon-de-Provence, the 20-year-old is just finding his international feet with 5 caps.
players.facts: Two Strasbourg teammates in the same Morocco squad — the Alsatian pipeline to the Atlas Lions is very real.

players.name: Amine Sbaï
players.bio: A Moroccan-born attacker plying his trade in Ligue 2 with Angers, Sbaï is a grafter with raw ambition. Only 2 caps in, his World Cup call-up is a testament to Morocco's vast talent pool and Sbaï's own persistence.
players.facts: Playing in France's second division and still earning a World Cup squad place. Dream big, kids.

players.name: Chadi Riad
players.full_name: Chadi Riad Dnanou; شادي رياض دنانو
players.bio: Born in Palma de Mallorca, Riad is a composed young centre-back who made his mark in Spain before earning a move to Crystal Palace. With 6 caps and a cheeky international goal already, the 22-year-old has a big future.
players.facts: Scored an international goal before turning 22 — not bad for a defender born on a Spanish holiday island.

players.name: Youssef Belammari
players.bio: A Rabat-born left-back who found his path through Egyptian football with Al Ahly, Belammari is dependable and energetic in defence. His 19 caps reflect steady progress in a competitive position for Morocco.
players.facts: He plays club football in Egypt with one of Africa's most decorated clubs — Al Ahly have won the African Champions League 13 times.

players.name: Ayoub El Kaabi
players.bio: Morocco's most prolific striker with a stunning 35 goals in 71 caps, El Kaabi is a natural-born goalscorer. The Casablanca native, now at Olympiacos, is a fox in the box who simply loves scoring — at every level.
players.facts: El Kaabi was the top scorer at the 2024 Africa Cup of Nations with 4 goals — and he's still hungry for more.

players.name: Ayoube Amaimouni
players.bio: Born in Vic, Spain, Amaimouni is a young winger who has made a bright start at Eintracht Frankfurt. With only 2 caps, he's very much at the beginning of his Atlas Lions journey — but what a stage to grow up on.
players.facts: Playing in the Bundesliga at 21 and already representing Morocco internationally. Not a bad two years at all.

players.name: Ahmed Reda Tagnaouti
players.bio: The third-choice keeper at this tournament, Tagnaouti plays for Moroccan military club AS FAR and has served the national team with quiet professionalism across 3 caps. A dependable presence in the squad.
players.facts: He plays for AS FAR — a club run by Morocco's Royal Armed Forces. Possibly the most disciplined dressing room in football.

players.name: Bilal El Khannouss
players.bio: A Belgian-born playmaker of Moroccan descent, El Khannouss is one of the most technically gifted young players in the squad. Now at Stuttgart, the 22-year-old magician has 3 international goals and an enormous ceiling.
players.facts: El Khannouss was born the same year Morocco last appeared in a World Cup knockout stage before 2022. Poetic timing.

players.name: Neil El Aynaoui
players.bio: A stylish, progressive midfielder who joined Roma and immediately caught the eye of national coach. With 2 goals in 16 caps, Neil El Aynaoui is building a reputation as one of Morocco's most promising midfield talents.
players.facts: Plays in Serie A for Roma and the international stage — not bad for a 24-year-old still gathering caps.

players.name: Redouane Halhal
players.bio: A young Belgian-based defender still in the early stages of his international career, Halhal brings athleticism and defensive intelligence from his time at Mechelen. Just 3 caps in — this World Cup could change everything.
players.facts: Halhal is one of very few players at WC 2026 whose club plays in Belgium's first division. Mechelen represent!

players.name: Anass Salah-Eddine
players.bio: A Dutch-born right-back making his way through PSV's set-up, Salah-Eddine is a technically assured defender with European flair. Nine caps in, he's gaining experience fast and could be a key depth option throughout the tournament.
players.facts: Two PSV Eindhoven players in the Morocco squad — Salah-Eddine and Saibari. The Eindhoven pipeline is flowing.
